Heat: rbk-mvp-3-release-qualification (₣BB) [racing]
Paddock: .claude/jjm/jjp_uBuB.md

Next: yoke-domain-shift-all-vessels (₢BBAA6) [rough]

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Discrete contract reshape — yoke domain shifts vessel→reliquary. Mechanically intricate but design is locked. Bundled orphan vessel cleanup closes the `rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels` test thread carrying-forward since BBAA3.
  
  ## Locked decisions
  
  - `rbw-dY` channel: `param1` → `""` (no folio); operation iterates every vessel under `${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/*/` with rbrv.env, writes RBRV_RELIQUARY into each
  - Primitive does **not** git commit — leaves changed files in repo dir
  - Orchestrator (inscribe-reliquary case in onboarding-sequence) **must** commit at end-of-case
  - Frontispiece: `DirectorYokesReliquaryInVessel` → `DirectorYokesReliquaryAllVessels`
  - BCG-compliant unimpeachable error handling — pre-validate reliquary stamp before touching any vessel; vessel-write failures surface clear diagnostic identifying which vessel failed and which were already written
  - Orphan vessel cleanup (bundled): delete `rbev-busybox-airgap-negative-canary`, `rbev-busybox-native-verify`, `rbev-sentry-deb-airgap`, `rbev-ubu-safety`
  
  ## Done criteria
  
  - `rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels` passes; fast suite 26/26
  - `tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh` green
  - Build clean under `#![deny(warnings)]`
  - All callers of `rbw-dY` updated; spec, tabtarget filename, zipper enrollment, frontispiece consistent
  
  ## Discovery recipes
  
  - Caller audit: `grep -rn "rbw-dY\|RBZ_YOKE_RELIQUARY\|YokesReliquaryInVessel" Tools/ tt/`
  - Per-vessel yoke-list residue in onboarding: `RBTDRO_YOKE_VESSEL_DIRS` const and consuming call sites in `rbtdro_onboarding.rs`
  - Spec: `RBSDY-director_yoke.adoc` — rewrite to reliquary-domain semantics
  - Witness probe: `RBTDRO_WITNESS_VESSEL_DIR` may need adjustment if witness vessel is now wildcard-discovered
  - Tabtarget rename: `tt/rbw-dY.DirectorYokesReliquaryInVessel.sh` → `tt/rbw-dY.DirectorYokesReliquaryAllVessels.sh` (filesystem rename + executable bit)
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - Validator (`rbrv_regime.sh:48`) mode-awareness rework — wildcard-yoke renders this latent, not load-bearing
  - Orphan vessel restoration — these directories are deleted, not preserved


Recommended: `jjx_arm` ₢₢BBAA6 or mount BB

91a75146  ₢BBAAg    Final assembly of the gauntlet test suite — rbw-tP release-qualification entry point. Composes the 11-fixture gauntlet ladder (enrollment-validation → pristine-lifecycle → canonical-establish → onboarding-sequence → regime-validation → regime-smoke → four-mode → tadmor → moriah → srjcl → pluml) as a TestSuite-shaped composition over existing infrastructure: ZRBTE_SUITE_GAUNTLET bash array in rbte_engine.sh + gauntlet) resolver branch + tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.gauntlet.sh launcher. The bash for-loop in rbte_suite() under set -e provides the suite-level fail-fast contract for free; no orchestrator-script needed. rbw-tP wired via rbz_zipper RBZ_QUALIFY_PRISTINE enrollment + rbq_qualify_pristine handler, which delegates contract enforcement to pristine-lifecycle case 1 (the entry-contract gate landed in BBAAB checks tree-clean + RBRR-blank + RBRA-absent + nameplate-hallmarks-empty + vessel-depot-fields-empty in one aggregated diagnostic). Docket prescribed dual enforcement at the rbw-tP layer plus pristine-lifecycle case 1, but BBAAB's final landed implementation explicitly omitted a commit-signature primitive ("no signature trailer") and the 5 violation classes are already enforced exhaustively as the gauntlet's first content fixture; duplicating them in bash would re-implement Rust predicates for cosmetic earlier-failure with maintenance-drift hazard. Operator running rbw-tP without marshal-zero sees enrollment-validation pass quickly (state-indifferent preflight) then pristine-lifecycle case 1 surface the violation diagnostic. Moriah included as a tadmor twin per operator direction: registered RBTDRM_FIXTURE_MORIAH + RBTDRC_FIXTURE_MORIAH sharing RBTDRC_SECTIONS_SECURITY (renamed from RBTDRC_SECTIONS_TADMOR) — runtime semantics identical to tadmor; provenance differs (cloud-built airgap vs local kludge). Section names stripped of "tadmor-" prefix to make the array genuinely shareable: 8 sections renamed (basic-infra, ifrit-attacks, observation, correlated, sortie-attacks, unilateral-novel, coordinated-attacks, coordinated-integrity). Theurge-ifrit-context doc table updated with new prefix-free names plus prose noting tadmor+moriah share the array. Build clean under #![deny(warnings)]; fast suite 25/26 with rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing failure unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4/BBAAs/BBAAv baseline; rbw-tf qualify-fast green; tt/rbtd-s.FixtureCase.moriah.sh now passes manifest verification (responds with vessel-exists precondition surfacing per BBAAs as expected). Tabtarget context regenerated via rbw-MG. Out of scope per docket: BBAAX first-run end-to-end validation, BBAAH pristine-passed sentinel, BBAAI documentation updates beyond what the workbench tabtarget needs. Pre-existing bug not addressed (out of scope, awaiting decision): rbq_qualify_release at rbq_Qualify.sh:183 references tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.complete.sh which doesn't exist — should be tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.complete.sh; would currently fail rbw-tr execution, but rbw-tr presumably hasn't been invoked since the rename. 5c833f5d  ₢BBAAv    Promote operation-name vocabulary to global rbtdrm_manifest registry: atomic RBTDRM_OPERATION_{INSCRIBE,YOKE,ORDAIN,ENSHRINE,KLUDGE} verbs and RBTDRM_CONTAINER_{SENTRY,BOTTLE} roles. Extract rbtdro_invoke_or_fail helper folding the duplicated invoke + exit-check pattern across six call sites (~50 lines collapsed). Compound "kludge sentry"/"kludge bottle" composed at call site from atomic constants. Enshrine invocation-error now includes vessel sigil (harmonized). Build clean under #![deny(warnings)]; fast suite 25/26 (pre-existing rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels unchanged). ef1ed323  ₢BBAAv    Replace four lowercase "yoke" string literals in rbtdro_onboarding.rs with a single RBTDRO_OPERATION_YOKE constant — error formats, label prefix, and inscribe-reliquary commit message all consume one source. RCG named-string discipline; build clean, fast suite 25/26 (pre-existing rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels unchanged). eba8f52f  ₢BBAAs    RCG named-string repair on the prior notch's BURE_CONFIRM bypass introduction. Operator flagged the 6 sites in rbtdrc_crucible.rs that read `&[("BURE_CONFIRM", "skip")]` as RCG-violating magic strings (per Tools/vok/vov_veiled/RCG-RustCodingGuide.md the codebase requires named string constants for env-var keys and protocol values). Prior precedent already existed: rbtdrp_pristine.rs:106-107 had module-private `const RBTDRP_BURE_CONFIRM_KEY: &str = "BURE_CONFIRM"` + `const RBTDRP_BURE_CONFIRM_SKIP: &str = "skip"` consumed at line 1030 — same primitive, but module-prefixed and not shared. Adding a second module's worth of magic strings would have created a duplicate-constants smell across rbtdrp_/rbtdrc_, so consolidated by extraction: defined `pub const RBTDRI_BURE_CONFIRM_KEY: &str = "BURE_CONFIRM"` and `pub const RBTDRI_BURE_CONFIRM_SKIP: &str = "skip"` in rbtdri_invocation.rs (the natural home: rbtdri_invoke_global is the primitive that propagates env-pairs to tabtargets, and rbtdri_invocation.rs already exports RBTDRI_BURV_OUTPUT_SUBDIR and RBTDRI_IFRIT_BINARY using the same pattern). Updated rbtdrc_crucible.rs (6 sites) and rbtdrp_pristine.rs (1 site at line 1025; was line 1030 before the dead-constant deletion) to import and use the new shared constants. Removed the rbtdrp_-private duplicates (4 lines including the docstring) since the shared rbtdri_ versions now serve as the single source of truth across the rbtd crate. Verification: tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh green (0.51s, no warnings); tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 with rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing failure unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4/BBAAs/this-officium-prior-notch baseline; grep `\"BURE_CONFIRM\"\|\"skip\"` confirms no remaining magic-string sites of these values in src/. Out of scope this notch (would expand the patch surface beyond what the operator review identified): broader audit of other env-var literals in rbtd/src/ such as RBTDRI_IFRIT_BINARY-pattern strings used elsewhere, and possible pull-up of rbtdri_invocation.rs's BURV_OUTPUT_SUBDIR alongside other dispatch-side conventions (BURD_BUK_DIR, BURD_TOOLS_DIR keys consumed by spawned tabtargets) into a unified env-key constants table. Discovery recipe for residual magic strings: `grep -rn '\\"[A-Z_]\\+\\"' Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/ | grep -v '//\\|test_'` to enumerate uppercase string literals that look like env keys or protocol tokens, then evaluate per RCG whether each warrants extraction — deferred since today's empirical loop is the active priority.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
3eda4229  ₢BBAAs    Two orthogonal fixes for the BBAAs onboarding-sequence empirical loop, landed together. (A) Three-line nudge in rbob_bottle.sh: at the three internal callsites (rbob_kludge:503, rbob_kludge_sentry:534, rbob_ordain:565) replace `rbfd_kludge "${z_vessel_dir}"` with `BUZ_FOLIO="${RBRN_*_VESSEL}" rbfd_kludge` (and the ordain analog). Drops the now-dead `local -r z_vessel_dir="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${RBRN_*_VESSEL}"` plus its `test -d` guard at each site (existence check moves into rbfd's own `zrbfc_resolve_vessel`, single source of truth for vessel resolution diagnostics). Solves kludge-tadmor blocker observed in today's empirical run: trace `Tools/rbk/rbfc_FoundryCore.sh: line 185: rbrv_list_capture: command not found` was symptom of `zrbfc_resolve_vessel(BUZ_FOLIO="tadmor")` falling through to listing branch because `tadmor` is the nameplate moniker (inherited from rbw-cKS dispatch into rbob_cli.sh process), not a vessel sigil — rbfd_kludge expected sigil/path. Bash assignment-prefix `VAR=value func` is the language's primitive for scoped env override and is symmetric with the dispatcher's `BUZ_FOLIO="${z_folio}" exec` (buz_zipper.sh:280) — same primitive at finer scope. Preserves BBAAs's universal-BUZ_FOLIO-contract design intent (handlers read BUZ_FOLIO uniformly across all rb*_, jjfp_, buhw_, rbgg_ modules; cli.sh furnishes also read BUZ_FOLIO for kindle); the rbob → rbfd internal calls just needed to honor the contract by setting BUZ_FOLIO to rbob's natural-derivative (RBRN_*_VESSEL sigil) for the inner call. After this patch, BUZ_FOLIO usage is uniform: dispatcher writes once via exec, callers may scope-override via assignment-prefix, all consumers read. Zero handler/dispatcher edits needed. (B) Strip --force flag from rbfl_jettison and rbfl_abjure plus their RBSIJ/RBSAA specs and 6 Rust orchestrator callsites in rbtdrc_crucible.rs — origin trace: docket revision 260128-2116 for ₢APAAT (silks `add-image-delete-by-ark`) introduced 'Confirm before deletion or require --force flag' as freelance docket text never operator-authorized; subsequent reslating preserved it; alignment commit 525b7fac (`:A:` Feb 9 2026) pulled `(add confirmation/force)` into the spec; bd344f92 (Haiku sub-agent, same day) implemented z_force/z_skip_confirm/--force doc-param mechanically; 388dd454 carried it through the rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh extraction; 7167b795 (BBAAs) included `${2:-}` → `${1:-}` shift specifically because of --force. Removed: `local z_force="${1:-}"`, `buc_doc_param "--force" "..."`, `local z_skip_confirm=false` + `if test "${z_force}" = "--force"` branch, and the `if test "${z_skip_confirm}" = "false"` wrapping around `buc_require` — `buc_require` runs unconditionally (single coherent path). The codebase already had a non-load-bearing-correct skip mechanism (`BURE_CONFIRM=skip` env var checked at buc_require:335-338, buc_command.sh) — --force was a redundant duplicate variant. Updated all 6 Rust orchestrator callsites in rbtdrc_crucible.rs (lines 2603, 2688, 2720, 2820, 2948, 3003) from `&[&hallmark, "--force"], &[]` to `&[&hallmark], &[("BURE_CONFIRM", "skip")]` — fixtures continue to auto-confirm via the existing env-var bypass; --force literal disappears from the entire rbk tree (verified via grep). Spec edits: RBSIJ-image_jettison.adoc:51-59 collapses 'Unless --force flag is provided' nested-step into unconditional 'Confirm Jettison' step; RBSAA-ark_abjure.adoc:84-92 same shape. Verification: bash -n green on rbob_bottle.sh and rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh; tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh green (theurge dev profile, 1.76s, no Rust warnings); tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh green (tabtargets, RBW colophon registrations, generated context freshness all OK); tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 with rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4/BBAAs baseline (carrying-forward marshal-zero validator surfacing) — no new failures introduced. Empirical re-run of onboarding-sequence pending operator go-ahead per docket iteration loop. Deferred follow-ups (separate notches when relevant): (i) source rbrv_regime.sh in zrbob_furnish to make rbrv_list_capture available in failure-listing branches reached from rbob processes (currently latent, surfaces only on resolution-failure paths and obscures real diagnostics); (ii) audit other Rust orchestrator/handbook callers passing positional args to BUZ_FOLIO-reading handlers — discovery recipe `grep -rn "rbtdri_invoke_global\|rbtdri_invoke_global_imprint" Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/` cross-referenced against zipper channel column for any handler with internal bash callers in rb*.sh modules. Caveat: scoped to the empirical failure trace and the unauthorized-flag scrub; if any handler outside this bug class has a similar internal-caller mismatch, will surface on the next empirical run as a new failure to diagnose.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 
7167b795  ₢BBAAs    Sweep rbw-* zipper channels from "" to "param1" and refactor handlers to read BUZ_FOLIO for the 14 enrollments whose orchestrator/operator-facing call shape passes a positional folio (vessel/hallmark/locator/stamp). Empirical post-BBAAs onboarding-sequence run (5/8 PASSED) hit `rbfd_enshrine: Vessel argument required (sigil or path)` on ordain-airgap's enshrine of rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge, with the diagnostic `WARNING: Colophon 'rbw-dE' takes no folio; ignoring unexpected argument(s): rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge` confirming the dispatcher stripped the arg before rbfd_enshrine's `${1:-}` could read it. Root cause: concurrent commit 17b3f85e (₢A-AAj, landed 2026-05-02 12:28:38 mid-fixture-run) tightened buz_exec_lookup's empty-channel branch from silent-pass-through to warn-and-clear ("Friendly-error UX extension: empty-channel commands now warn-and-clear when given unexpected positional args instead of silently passing them through to the formulary") to surface bogus args to truly-no-folio commands like buw-rnl. The change was correct in intent but unsynchronized: many rbw-* enrollments declared channel `""` while their handlers in rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh, rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh, rbfr_FoundryRetriever.sh, and rbfc_FoundryCore.sh read `${1:-}` for a folio they had been receiving via the prior silent-pass-through. ordain-conjure (case 4) passed because it ran at 12:03:45 — pre-17b3f85e — under the old behavior; enshrine-upstream ran at 12:30:13 — post-17b3f85e — under the new behavior and died on empty `${1:-}`. Fix shape mirrors the prior yoke fix (commit 4097610f, BBAAs): change channel `""` to `"param1"` in zipper enrollment, change handler's folio read from `${1:-}` to `${BUZ_FOLIO:-}`, and (for handlers with positional --force flag) shift the flag read from `${2:-}` to `${1:-}` since the dispatcher now consumes the folio and forwards remaining args. Affected colophons (orchestrator-passes-args set, identified via grep of rbtdri_invoke_global call sites in Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs and rbtdro_onboarding.rs): rbw-dE (rbfd_enshrine, vessel), rbw-fO (rbfd_ordain, vessel), rbw-fk (rbfd_kludge, vessel), rbw-fA (rbfl_abjure, hallmark + --force), rbw-fs (rbfr_summon, hallmark), rbw-fpf/rbw-fpc (rbfc_plumb_full/compact, hallmark), rbw-irh/rbw-irr (rbfl_rekon_hallmark/reliquary, hallmark/stamp), rbw-iwh/rbw-iwr/rbw-iwe (rbfl_wrest, locator), rbw-iJh/rbw-iJr/rbw-iJe (rbfl_jettison, locator + --force) — 14 enrollments, 11 distinct handlers (3 wrest + 3 jettison enrollments share their handlers). Excluded from sweep (genuinely no-folio, channel `""` correct): rbw-aM mantle, rbw-arr/rbw-adr roster, rbw-dL/dU/dl depot ops, rbw-dI inscribe, rbw-gPI/PE/PR/q payor, rbw-Occ/Ocr/Ocd/Ofc/Ots/Odf/Oda/Odb/Odg/Op/Og/o onboarding handbooks, rbw-iah/iar/iae audit, rbw-MZ/MP/MG marshal, rbw-ft tally, rbw-fV vouch — these handlers take no folio and the new warn-and-clear behavior catches operator typos correctly. BCG note on furnish: unlike the rbfl_yoke fix which moved zrbrv_kindle to rbfl_cli.sh's furnish per BCG "kindle is CLI-only" rule (yoke needed to write to a kindle constant RBRV_RELIQUARY before validation), the handlers in this sweep call `zrbfc_resolve_vessel`/`zrbfc_load_vessel` (pure source, no kindle) or consume hallmark/locator/stamp strings (no regime state at all), so reading BUZ_FOLIO from the function body is BCG-conformant and no CLI furnish changes are needed in rbfd_cli.sh, rbfl_cli.sh, rbfr_cli.sh, or rbfc_cli.sh. The dispatcher's `BUZ_FOLIO="${z_folio}" exec ...` (buz_zipper.sh:280) makes the folio available as an inherited environment variable to the function body. Tabtarget context regenerated via tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh — the Folio column for the 14 affected rows flips from `—` (empty channel) to `param1`, satisfying the qualify-fast freshness gate. Verification: bash -n green on all 5 edited shell files; tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh green (theurge dev profile); tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 with rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4/BBAAs baseline (carrying-forward marshal-zero validator surfacing); tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh passes — tabtarget structure, RBW colophon registrations, and generated context freshness all green. No new shellcheck warnings expected (pattern-mechanical refactor, no new variable patterns or constructs introduced). Empirical re-run of onboarding-sequence pending operator go-ahead per docket iteration loop. Caveat: this sweep covers the orchestrator-passes-args set; if a handler outside that set (e.g., bash-level callers in handbook prose using positional args to a `""`-channel tabtarget) was relying on the old silent-pass-through, it will now warn-and-clear and fail downstream. Discovery recipe for residual sites: grep for `${1:-}` reads in rbg*/rbf*/rbh*/rbl*/rbr*/rbo*/rb[a-z]_*.sh handler functions and cross-reference against zipper channel column for `""`. Out of scope for this notch (deferred per docket): full project-wide audit of every `""`-channel handler's `${1:-}`/`${2:-}` reads to flush remaining latent failures before they surface empirically.                    
768cc71c  ₣BB       BCG: codify executable-bit rule — _cli.sh must be +x, all other module *.sh must not. Adds Filesystem marker subsection to § CLI as Module Gateway, drops 'executable' from the line 41 file-map (now stated separately as a hard rule), and adds matching item to Module Maturity Checklist §
